Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Clicking on (31, 30) - the left edge of the right green - DID work! It changed 1 cell and gameplay_changed=True. This selected the right green for movement. Now let me try pressing RIGHT to move it.
But wait, the right green is at cols 30-32 and the purple bar is at cols 33-38. So pressing RIGHT would try to move the green into the purple bar, which blocks it. Earlier, when a green was blocked, it swapped center colors with the other green.
Let me try pressing RIGHT to see if the color swap still works.
Actually, let me first check what changed when I clicked on (31, 30). What cell changed?

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check what changed after clicking on (31, 30)
# Compare before and after frames
before = last_transition.before_frame
after = last_transition.after_frame

# Find differences
before_nodes = {n['boundary']: n for n in before.segmentation['nodes']}
after_nodes = {n['boundary']: n for n in after.segmentation['nodes']}

# Check green positions and centers
print("BEFORE greens:")
for n in before.segmentation['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'N':
        center = next((x for x in before.segmentation['nodes'] if x['id'] in n['children']), None)
        print(f"  boundary={n['boundary']}, center={center['color'] if center else 'none'}")

print("AFTER greens:")
for n in after.segmentation['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'N':
        center = next((x for x in after.segmentation['nodes'] if x['id'] in n['children']), None)
        print(f"  boundary={n['boundary']}, center={center['color'] if center else 'none'}")

# Now try RIGHT to move the selected green
action(['RIGHT'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
